untranslatable_reason: Signal requires direct measurement of market depth, bid-ask spread ratios, and participant concentration metrics over monthly periods with 30% degradation thresholds. These structural liquidity metrics are not available in the allowed indicator set. The signal also requires forward-looking projected performance (65% of historical baseline), which cannot be computed from price/technical indicators alone. Used vol_ratio_20 as a weak proxy for liquidity degradation, but this does not capture the signal's intent of measuring market microstructure changes.
translation_notes: This is a regime-detection rule requiring market microstructure analysis rather than price-action signals. The original signal emphasizes structural market changes (declining depth, widening spreads, concentration shifts) that make an edge obsolete—this is fundamentally a meta-strategy rule about when to exit a trading style entirely, not a price-level entry/exit signal. A proper implementation would require access to order book depth, bid-ask spread time series, and liquidity provider concentration data.
